title: Cameron Norrie Wins a Tough Tennis Match
summary: Cameron Norrie is a tennis player from Britain. He played a match in Madrid, Spain. He won against Tomas Machac, who is from the Czech Republic. The match was very exciting and close. Norrie won the first set easily. In the second set, he almost won but Machac fought back and won that set. In the third set, Norrie was losing at first, but he tried very hard and won the final tie-break. He said it was one of his favorite matches because the crowd was amazing and he had to work really hard. Now he will play another match against either Tommy Paul or Thiago Agustin Tirante. Another British player, Katie Boulter, lost her match. The world number one, Jannik Sinner, also won his match.

keywords.explanation: A part of a tennis match. A player must win six games to win a set.
A special game played when the score is tied at six games each in a set. The first player to win seven points wins the tie-break.
When a player wins a game while the other player is serving.

background_read: Tennis is a sport where two players hit a ball over a net. Matches are played in sets. A player wins a set by winning six games. If both players win six games, they play a tie-break. The player who wins the most sets wins the match.
Article_Structure: WHO: Cameron Norrie, a British tennis player
WHAT: He won a difficult match against Tomas Machac
WHERE: In Madrid, Spain at the Madrid Open
WHY: He played hard and never gave up, even when he was losing

perspectives.description: He was very happy and said it was one of his favorite matches.
He might be disappointed because he had chances to win but missed them.
